items.mistake: Fitting `StandardScaler`, imputers, or encoders on the full dataset before `train_test_split`
items.fix: Split first, then `fit` transformers on training rows only and `transform` validation and test.
items.why: Test distribution statistics leak into scaling and encoding parameters, inflating held-out scores.
items.example: `scaler.fit(X)` on all rows, then split → test rows influenced mean/std. Use `scaler.fit(X_train)` only.

items.mistake: Including target-encoded or aggregated features computed with knowledge of the label on the full table
items.fix: Compute encodings inside cross-validation folds or with out-of-fold schemes on training data only.
items.why: Target statistics from validation or test rows directly encode labels into features.
items.example: Global `mean(target) by category` before split lets rare test categories inherit label means from the full set.

items.mistake: Random row splits on panel data (users, patients, stores) with multiple rows per entity
items.fix: Use `GroupShuffleSplit`, `StratifiedGroupKFold`, or split by entity/time so no group appears in both train and test.
items.why: Near-duplicate rows across splits let the model memorize entities instead of generalizing.
items.example: User 42 has 30 rows: 20 in train and 10 in test → test rows are not independent of training.

items.mistake: Shuffling time-series data and using standard k-fold cross-validation
items.fix: Sort by time, use `TimeSeriesSplit` or rolling-origin validation, and build features with past-only windows.
items.why: Future rows in training leak information about trends and shocks that precede test periods.
items.example: Predict Monday sales using Friday's actual weekend promo outcome that was decided after Monday.
